steps.title: Identify the endpoint values and their trend
steps.content: In 1910, 123,990 cars were produced by 320 companies. In 1925, 3,185,881 cars were produced by 80 companies. The key signal: production soared while the number of companies plummeted.

steps.title: Recognize the inverse relationship in the choices
steps.content: Choice A correctly captures both movements: increased production alongside decreased companies. Choices B, C, and D contradict the data—claiming no change (B), declining cars (C), or companies rising (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
